metadata.title: Why Summarizing Deletes Exactly the Caveats

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Namaste, Liam — in for Bear. Zero caveats? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.segment: Why Summarizing Deletes Exactly the Caveats

beats.narration_text: A twelve-page grant progress report. Six caveats. A projection that is not an outcome. One executive summary. Zero caveats.

card.copy: Why Summarizing Deletes Exactly the Caveats

beats.narration_text: The executive summary looks authoritative. It sounds confident. It is shorter by design — and everything it dropped was the part that wasn't certain.
beats.scene_description: a neatly formatted one-page executive summary document on a desk — looks clean, professional, authoritative
beats.image_prompt: B02, close-up of a clean one-page executive summary document on a desk, professional formatting with section headers, looks authoritative and polished, editorial collage style, desaturated newsprint treatment, warm cream tones, no faces visible

beats.narration_text: Summarizing should shrink a document roughly evenly. Instead it kept every confident claim and dropped every caveat. Why that selection?

document.quote: Summarizing should shrink a document roughly evenly. Instead it kept every confident claim and dropped every caveat. Why that selection?

beats.narration_text: Here is the source report — twelve pages. Six confident claims. Six caveats attached to those claims. Both are there. Both look like text.

viz.label: vertical stack: 6 solid TEAL blocks (claims) each paired with a thin CRIMSON block below (caveats); label '12-page report'
viz.note: the source report — claims and caveats present together

beats.narration_text: Here is the compression. A bar descends. The caveat blocks are thin — they read as peripheral. They go first. The claim blocks are solid. They stay.

viz.label: same stack; a SLATE bar descends from above; CRIMSON thin blocks disappear; TEAL solid blocks remain; label '1-page summary'

beats.narration_text: Caveats, uncertainty, and minority findings read as peripheral. Confident claims read as essential. A summarizer optimized for coherence and brevity cuts what appears least central — and caveats always appear least central.

viz.label: two columns: KEPT (TEAL — confident, clear, central) and DROPPED (CRIMSON — caveats, uncertainty, peripheral); arrow from 'compression' pointing to KEPT column only
viz.note: mechanism: compression selects for confident claims

beats.narration_text: What gets cut is not random. It tends to be caveats, uncertainty, and minority findings. Compression makes confident prose from source material that was never that certain.

document.quote: What gets cut is not random. It tends to be caveats, uncertainty, and minority findings. Compression makes confident prose from materials that were never that certain.

beats.narration_text: The summary that emerges is shorter, cleaner, and more confident than the source. It was made from the same source. The source was not that confident.

viz.label: left (SOURCE): text block with TEAL claim + CRIMSON caveat interleaved; right (SUMMARY): text block in TEAL only — caveat absent; arrow between
viz.note: before vs after: caveat gone, claim unchanged

beats.narration_text: The folder looks complete. The summary reads authoritative. The projection that became a result is invisible.
beats.scene_description: a laptop screen showing a polished executive summary with professional formatting — clean, complete-looking, nothing obviously wrong
beats.image_prompt: B10, close-up of a laptop screen showing a polished executive summary with clean formatting and professional headers, looks complete and authoritative, editorial collage style, desaturated newsprint treatment, cream and charcoal tones, no faces visible

beats.narration_text: Illustrative. Six claims. Six caveats in the source. The summary kept all six claims. The summary dropped all six caveats. One sentence read 'engagement is projected to exceed targets.' The summary reads 'engagement exceeded targets.'

viz.label: SOURCE (TEAL): 'engagement is projected to exceed targets'; arrow; SUMMARY (CRIMSON): 'engagement exceeded targets'; below: '6 claims kept · 0 caveats kept'; 'illustrative'

beats.narration_text: Six claims, six caveats going in. Six claims, zero caveats coming out. The document got shorter. The uncertainty got erased.

viz.label: IN: 6 TEAL blocks (claims) + 6 CRIMSON blocks (caveats); OUT: 6 TEAL blocks + 0 CRIMSON blocks; 'claims: 6→6 · caveats: 6→0'
viz.note: the tally — what went in vs what came out

beats.narration_text: Compression preserves the impressive. It does not preserve the accurate.

document.quote: Compression preserves the impressive. It does not preserve the accurate.

beats.narration_text: The summary kept every claim. It dropped every caveat.

card.copy: The summary kept every claim. It dropped every caveat.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Compression preserves the impressive. It does not preserve the accurate. The summary kept every claim. It dropped every caveat.

props.artifactHeading: Why Summarizing Deletes Exactly the Caveats
artifactLines: Six claims, six caveats going in.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why Summarizing Deletes Exactly the Caveats] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
